tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Fix Azure.GeneratorAgent CHANGELOG and README gaps after MCP server refactor (#57939)","author":{"name":"Copilot","email":"198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-08T21:58:23Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-08T21:58:23Z"}},"author":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"fix(ServiceBus): Read max-message-batch-size vendor property for batch sizing (#57941)

* fix(ServiceBus): Read max-message-batch-size vendor property for batch sizing

- Read com.microsoft:max-message-batch-size from AMQP sender link
  properties to correctly limit batch size on Premium large-message
  entities where max-message-size can be up to 100 MB but the batch
  limit is 1 MB
- Remove hardcoded 4,500 message count cap; the service has no
  count limit and the byte size limit naturally caps message count
- Falls back to DefaultMaxBatchSize (1 MB) when vendor property
  is absent (backward compatibility)
- Add unit tests for vendor property scenarios (Premium large-message,
  Standard tier, absent fallback, count cap removal)

* fix: address Copilot review - robust count cap test

- Use 10 MB batch limit in count cap test to ensure all 6000 messages
  fit regardless of AMQP envelope overhead variations

* fix: address jsquire review - simplify comment, null-conditional, merge test helper

- Trim verbose vendor property comment to avoid staleness
- Use link.Settings?.Properties.TryGetValue instead of explicit null check
- Merge SetLinkLimitsWithBatchSize into SetLinkLimits with optional maxBatchSize param

* docs: add CHANGELOG entry for batch size vendor property fix

---------

Co-authored-by: Eldert Grootenboer (from Dev Box) <egrootenboer@microsoft.com>","author":{"name":"Eldert Grootenboer","email":"eldert@eldert.net","date":"2026-04-08T21:38:06Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-08T21:38:06Z"}},"author":{"login":"EldertGrootenboer","id":19503614,"profile_url":"https://github.com/EldertGrootenboer","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19503614?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":" [KeyVault] Migrate or retire stand-alone samples (#57907)

* [KeyVault] Migrate or retire stand-alone samples

 The focus of these chagnes is to move `getcert` and
 `keyvaultproxy` samples from `sdk/keyvault/samples/`
 to the centralized `samples/keyvault/` directory.
 The the sharelink sample is being retired as it is
 for a legacy feature that goes against Microsoft
 security guidance and has no updates planned.

 Details:
 - Move getcert and keyvaultproxy samples to samples/keyvault/
 - Retire sharelink (deprecated KV-managed storage accounts feature)
 - Delete sdk/keyvault/samples/ build infrastructure
 - Strip IsSample/IsSamplesProject conditional blocks from csproj files
 - Bump Azure.Identity, Azure.Core, KV.Certificates, KV.Secrets,
   System.CommandLine, xunit, and test SDK to latest stable versions
 - Rename getcert.csproj to GetCert.csproj for C# naming conventions
 - Update stale sample links in TROUBLESHOOTING.md, MigrationGuide.md,
   and KeyVaultCertificate.cs to point to new locations
 - Remove keyvaultproxy test entry from splittestdependencies inputs
 - Update READMEs: .NET Core 3.1 references to .NET 10

* Fixing sample links; the sample browser will publish to the existing locations, so the links are safe","author":{"name":"Jesse Squire","email":"jesse.squire@gmail.com","date":"2026-04-08T20:33:46Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-08T20:33:46Z"}},"author":{"login":"jsquire","id":913445,"profile_url":"https://github.com/jsquire","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Sync eng/common directory with azure-sdk-tools for PR 14918 (#57880)

Co-authored-by: Scott Beddall <scbedd@microsoft.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-08T19:44:54Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-08T19:44:54Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Fix race condition in GetAsync between terminal status and persistence (#57899)

* Fix race condition in GetAsync between terminal status and persistence

When a background response reaches terminal status during event processing,
GetAsync could return the completed snapshot before FinalizeExecutionAsync
called UpdateResponseAsync. Add a wait gate in GetAsync that awaits the
ExecutionTask when status is terminal but CompletedAt is null.

Fixes #57893

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky test: BgMode_ProviderHasOneCreateAndOneUpdate race condition

GET returning a terminal status does not guarantee that finalization
(persistence via UpdateResponseAsync) has completed. Add a poll helper
that waits for the expected spy call before asserting, making the test
resilient to the race between observable status and persistence.

Fixes #57893

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert product code change — move fix to test level

The service team confirmed GetAsync behavior is by design. The test
must be resilient to the race between observable status and persistence
rather than gating GetAsync on finalization completion.

* [EngSys] Agentic triage routing report

The focus of these changes is to add a section to
the agentic triage metadata to report on the analysis
and decision made for routing the issue to an owner.
This is informational only and does not influence the
routing decision itself.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Dapeng Zhang","email":"dapzhang@microsoft.com","date":"2026-04-08T07:15:23Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-08T07:15:23Z"}},"author":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"profile_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Add convenience builders and restructure samples for progressive complexity (#57895)

* Update stale spec rule references to formalized numbering

Replace all retired FR-xxx functional requirement codes and
repurposed S-047/S-048 references with the formalized rule numbers:

- S-047 (old: Response-ID resolution) → B38
- S-048 (old: Session-ID resolution) → B39
- FR-001..003 (SSE headers) → B27
- FR-004..015 (tracing) → §7.2/§7.3/S-044
- FR-006..011 (handler validation) → S-031/S-033/B30/S-015/B32
- FR-012/013 (bg resilience) → B18
- FR-014 (store=false) → B14; (cancel) → B11/S-024
- FR-016/017 (startup) → S-004; (TTL) → S-038/B35
- FR-019..022 (RawBody) → S-005
- FR-020/021 (keep-alive) → B28

Also update rule range ceilings in AGENTS.md and
doc-ownership-matrix.md: B1–B37 → B1–B39, S-001–S-045/046 → S-001–S-052.
Add new Layer 2 rows for §15 Convenience Helpers and §16 Upstream Interop.

* Restructure samples for progressive complexity with convenience-first pattern

- Sample 3: Add convenience-first handler (OutputItemMessage), streaming
  convenience handler (IAsyncEnumerable<string> overload), and rename
  builder handler to GreetingHandlerFullControl
- Sample 4: Add convenience-first handler (OutputItemFunctionCall +
  OutputItemMessage), rename builder handler to WeatherHandlerFullControl
- Sample 6: Add convenience-first handler (OutputItemReasoningItem +
  OutputItemMessage), rename builder handler to MathSolverHandlerFullControl
- Update 'With full event control' descriptions to accurately reflect when
  builders are needed (multiple content parts, refusal, custom properties,
  interleaving work) vs. when conveniences suffice (including streaming)
- Add E2E tests for all new sample handlers (7 new tests, 23 total)
- Update handler-implementation-guide.md builder description
- Update samples README descriptions
- Run full pre-commit checks (Export-API, Update-Snippets, BuildSnippets,
  dotnet format)

* Update AGENTS.md docs, fix sample reference, and enhance Bootstrap-Copilot script

- Add E2E sample test requirements and continuous learning governance to AGENTS.md
- Update Responses AGENTS.md with clarified spec rule references
- Fix Sample3 markdown snippet reference
- Enhance Bootstrap-Copilot.ps1 with improved spec cloning and validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused using System.Text from ResponseEventStream

Addresses review feedback: unused using would fail build with CS8019
(TreatWarningsAsErrors).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix broken relative link in Responses AGENTS.md

Replace relative ../AGENTS.md with absolute GitHub URL to satisfy
CI link verification check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

- Move tsp-location.yaml from src/TypeSpec/ to the package root
  (sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/) to match repo
  convention used by all other TypeSpec-based packages.

  package root as --output-dir (where tsp-location.yaml now lives).

arguments.message: No documentation gaps found. The triggering push (2452ce22, PR #58002) synced eng/common scripts (Verify-Readme.ps1, deps installation). Other recent commits were automated version bumps (Azure.Provisioning.MySql, Azure.ResourceManager.Sql), a ToolsVersion removal across build files, and a TypeSpec package-lock update — none of which introduce new public APIs or SDK features requiring documentation updates.
